summary: Tested inline REACH Assessment widget embedded in G0-6 panel on Ground 0 page (/ground-0-briefing). Full end-to-end flow tested and passed.

issues: Minor React console warning: mixing shorthand 'padding' with 'paddingTop' in inline styles causes style rerender conflict (non-blocking)

critical_code_review_comments: Minor: REACHAssessmentWidget uses both 'padding' and 'paddingTop' in the same style object for the box variable and the 'analyzing' phase — causes a harmless React style warning. Can be fixed by using explicit paddingTop/paddingBottom/paddingLeft/paddingRight instead of shorthand.
context_for_next_testing_agent: REACH Assessment widget in G0-6 fully working. All 12 feature checkpoints passed: page load, G0-6 accordion, Begin REACH Assessment button, widget intro/start, 15-question flow (including insight pauses), open text Q15, analyzing animation, GO/WAIT/NO-GO result display with Risk Map, email capture, post-submit CTA, and other panel accordion toggling.
tests_executed: All 6 module panels (G0-1 through G0-6) present
G0-6 accordion opens and shows Begin REACH Assessment button (data-testid='g0-begin-reach-btn')
Clicking Begin REACH Assessment shows widget with Run the Diagnostic button (data-testid='widget-reach-start')
All 14 MCQ answered sequentially with auto-advance
Submitting open text triggered analyzing animation then results
Result phase: OPERATIONAL READINESS: GO badge, headline, 3 bullets, Authority Risk Map all displayed
Email capture input (widget-email-input) present in results
G0-1 panel toggled successfully confirming other panels unaffected
